role_title: Senior Product Manager, AI Ecosystem Integrations

location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role/team

level_signals: 5-8+ years of product management experience required
Base salary range CAD $176K-$200K consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-8+ years of product management experience, with majority in building and growing user-facing features in B2B SaaS products serving Enterprise customers.
must_haves.qualifier: majority in B2B SaaS serving Enterprise customers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Systems thinker: You can conceptualize valuable connections between user actions and notifications across different channels, and understand how these things factor into user adoption and retention.
Genuine AI enthusiasm. You're curious, adaptable, and genuinely excited about how LLMs are transforming software discovery and adoption.
Strategic: You inspire the company by creating bold, intuitive work experiences for our users across different channels to best solve user problems.
Get Stuff Done: You create, flex, and evangelize a roadmap; thoughtfully break down projects to MVPs and iterative projects to ship with high velocity and business impact.
Customer-centric: You analyze our top customer personas; dig into the roots of customer needs; synthesize research to gain a deep understanding and narrow definition of a problem.

Communication and collaboration: You partner with cross-functional teammates to deliver high-quality results. You speak with clarity and write sharp product specs. You are highly comfortable getting into technical discussions with engineers about the pros and cons of different approaches, and you pair with design on complex user flows.
Growth Mindset: You lead with curiosity and are open to feedback in order to learn.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Candidates who've built on 3rd party platform integrations is nice-to-have.
Experience with AI/ML products, LLM APIs, is nice to have.

scope.surface: Communications Integrations — Asana on Google, Microsoft, and Slack platforms; Asana AI connections to Gemini, Copilot, and 3rd-party AIs/agents
primary_metrics_inferred: Reach of Asana workflows on partner platforms
core_responsibilities: Lead a team to build powerful, delightful experiences for users on Asana's top integrations on Google, Microsoft, and Slack to drive user adoption, retention, and growth

Partner closely with Google, Microsoft, and Slack to develop a shared roadmap that maximizes the reach of Asana workflows and the power of Asana AI, including how Asana's AI interacts with 3rd-party AI on these platforms
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders and synthesize data and user inputs to identify customer problems, establish a vision, set strategy, build and ship solutions
Partner with engineering, design, and other functions to execute on roadmaps with high velocity
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting goals and milestones

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of compensation package

green_flags: Clear product surface with named partners (Google, Microsoft, Slack)
Explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
Equity mentioned as part of total compensation
Asana named to Fortune's Best Workplaces for 7+ years
red_flags: Compensation stated in CAD — role is Vancouver-based, limiting applicant pool to Canada (or those willing to relocate)
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not stated
Office-centric hybrid with 3 mandatory in-office days per week (Mon, Tue, Thu) may be restrictive
No explicit USD equivalent provided for non-Canadian applicants to assess comp

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title and years range)

ambiguities: '5-8+ years' is an unusual range format — treated as minimum_value: 5 with no hard ceiling; the '+' after 8 suggests 8 is not a cutoff
